IMD Issues Yellow Alert for 20 Odisha Districts Ahead of Bahuda Yatra
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a Yellow Warning for 20 districts of Odisha, forecasting heavy rainfall, thunderstorms and gusty winds ahead of the Bahuda Yatra of Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra and Devi Subhadra.
According to the IMD, the weather pattern is expected to be similar to the rainfall witnessed during the Shree Gundicha Yatra, which may affect the return chariot festival in Puri.
Heavy Rainfall Alert
Heavy rainfall is likely at isolated places in:
The weather office has also forecast continued rainfall across northern and coastal Odisha on Tuesday and Wednesday, with Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar and Sundargarh likely to receive isolated heavy showers.
Thunderstorm Warning
A Yellow Warning for thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds (30–40 kmph) has been issued for:
Similar weather conditions are also expected in Cuttack, Kendrapara, Jagatsinghpur, Jajpur, Bhadrak and Balasore.
Seven-Day Weather Outlook
IMD Bhubaneswar Director Manorama Mohanty said light to moderate rainfall is likely across several parts of Odisha over the next seven days, while isolated areas may witness heavy to very heavy rainfall, especially in Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar and Sundargarh.
She added that several coastal districts are expected to experience rain accompanied by lightning, thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching 40–50 kmph at isolated locations.
Advisory for Bahuda Yatra Devotees
With lakhs of devotees expected to participate in Bahuda Yatra, the IMD has advised people to stay updated on weather forecasts, carry rain protection and follow the instructions of local authorities and disaster management officials during the festival.
